channel.title: The Cambridge Record — schools
channel.description: Everything on the record about schools.

item.description: The Finance Committee held a public hearing on Tuesday, May 12, 2026 on the City and School budgets covering the fiscal period July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2027.

item.title: CMA 2026-154 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a cross statutory transfer of $322,825.00 within
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a cross statutory transfer of $322,825.00 within the School Department General Fund.

item.title: POR 2026-99 — That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to work with the Mayor’s Office, Law Department, Election Commission, a
item.description: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to work with the Mayor’s Office, Law Department, Election Commission, and other relevant City Departments to engage the Collins Center in assisting the City in reviewing policy options for allowing Cambridge voters to directly elect the City’s Mayor.

item.title: POR 2026-66 — That the City Manager is requested to initiate the planning process for Cambridge 400 and as part of this, to convene a
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to initiate the planning process for Cambridge 400 and as part of this, to convene a Cambridge 400 Advisory Committee composed of members representing the range of stakeholders whose participation is needed to guide this work.

item.title: POR 2026-39 — THAT THE CITY MANAGER BE AND HEREBY IS REQUESTED TO INSTRUCT ALL CITY DEPARTMENTS, BOARDS, COMMISSIONS, AND OFFICES TO D
item.description: THAT THE CITY MANAGER BE AND HEREBY IS REQUESTED TO INSTRUCT ALL CITY DEPARTMENTS, BOARDS, COMMISSIONS, AND OFFICES TO DISCONTINUE ALL OFFICIAL POSTING AND ENGAGEMENT ON X WITHIN 60 DAYS; AND TO INCLUDE AN EXPLANATION OF WHY THE CITY WILL NO LONGER USE X.

item.title: POR 2026-34 — That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which C
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which Cambridge can expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming, and exploration of non-City funding sources, from the state or foundations. item.title: RES 2026-01 — That the City Council formally go on record in extending its deepest condolences to the family of Donald Williams for th
item.description: That the City Council formally go on record in extending its deepest condolences to the family of Donald Williams for their tremendous loss.

item.description: That the City Manager ask the appropriate departments to review the city’s digital equity work to date, including the study’s recommendations and all steps taken since the study conclusion in order to update the evaluation of existing internet access programs, assessing whether residents’ digital ne

item.description: Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $100,000, received from the Mass Broadband Institute to the Grant Fund Library Other Ordinary Maintenance account.
